chief_complaint: Four patients (two pediatric, two adult) presented with cardiogenic shock requiring venoarterial extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (V-A ECMO).
history: All patients were in cardiogenic shock necessitating V-A ECMO. Left ventricular distention was exacerbated by V-A ECMO, prompting the need for effective decompression strategies.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: ECG, bloods, and imaging studies were not specified, but echocardiography was used to guide the procedure.
correct_diagnosis: Left ventricular overload during V-A ECMO
differentials: Left ventricular overload during V-A ECMO
Venoarterial extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (V-A ECMO) failure
correct_ranking: Left ventricular overload during V-A ECMO
Venoarterial extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (V-A ECMO) failure
